headline: Google, Blackstone launch cloud company as Wall Street races to fund AI boom
summary: The two giants are launching an AI compute supplier as the demand for AI infrastructure continues to grow.

content.title: Google, Blackstone launch cloud company as Wall Street races to fund AI boom
content.summary: The two giants are launching an AI compute supplier as the demand for AI infrastructure continues to grow.

headline: Blackstone Announces Joint Venture with Google to Create New TPU Cloud
summary: NEW YORK, May 19, 2026--Blackstone (NYSE: BX) today announced a joint venture with Google to create a new U.S.-based company that will offer efficient data center capacity, operations, networking, and Google Cloud's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) as a compute-as-a-service offering. The company will give customers another option to access cloud TPUs in addition to using them through Google Cloud.

content.title: Blackstone Announces Joint Venture with Google to Create New TPU Cloud
content.summary: NEW YORK, May 19, 2026--Blackstone (NYSE: BX) today announced a joint venture with Google to create a new U.S.-based company that will offer efficient data center capacity, operations, networking, and Google Cloud's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) as a compute-as-a-service offering. The company will give customers another option to access cloud TPUs in addition to using them through Google Cloud.

headline: Google, Blackstone to launch AI cloud venture to meet data centre demand
summary: Alphabet's Google and Blackstone said on Monday that they will form an artificial ‌intelligence cloud business venture aimed at capitalising on an insatiable demand ‌for AI computing services. Blackstone, the world's largest alternative asset manager, will invest an initial $5 billion in ​equity to help bring 500 megawatts of data centre capacity online in 2027, with further expansion planned over time. The U.S.-based venture will provide data centre capacity along with Google's custom AI chips, known as Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, through ‌a compute-as-a-service model.

content.title: Google, Blackstone to launch AI cloud venture to meet data centre demand
content.summary: Alphabet's Google and Blackstone said on Monday that they will form an artificial ‌intelligence cloud business venture aimed at capitalising on an insatiable demand ‌for AI computing services. Blackstone, the world's largest alternative asset manager, will invest an initial $5 billion in ​equity to help bring 500 megawatts of data centre capacity online in 2027, with further expansion planned over time. The U.S.-based venture will provide data centre capacity along with Google's custom AI chips, known as Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, through ‌a compute-as-a-service model.

headline: SpaceX IPO Adds Second Musk Stock. It’s a Problem for Tesla
summary: (Bloomberg) -- For years, there was only one way for mom-and-pop investors to buy into Elon Musk’s vision: shares of Tesla Inc. That’s about to change — and it’s a serious risk for Tesla investors. headline: Omdia: Amazon, Netflix and Google to Capture Half of $81 Billion CTV Advertising Market by 2030
summary: LONDON, May 19, 2026--Global connected TV (CTV) advertising revenue will surge from $44 billion in 2025 to $81 billion by 2030, with CTV ad revenues expected to surpass traditional linear TV advertising during the 2030s, according to new research by Omdia.

headline: Apple’s Gemini Siri Overhaul Puts AI Privacy And Valuation In Focus
summary: Apple, ticker NasdaqGS:AAPL, is preparing a major Siri overhaul for iOS 27 that uses new AI capabilities and tighter privacy controls. The update is set to integrate Google Gemini AI within Apple’s ecosystem while emphasizing user control and automatic data minimization. This move marks a shift in how Apple approaches AI partnerships, virtual assistants and on-device privacy. For Apple, the company behind the iPhone, iPad and Mac, this kind of AI refresh is about more than a new software...

headline: The Trump Bull Market Has a Very Dark Side That Could End Badly for Investors
summary: The stock market has climbed a wall of worry since President Donald Trump returned to the White House on Jan. 20, 2025. Tariff fights rattled investors. Treasury yields spiked. Recession fears surfaced more than once. Yet the benchmark S&P 500 has still surged roughly 23.5% since Inauguration Day. That kind of resilience usually feels bullish. ... The Trump Bull Market Has a Very Dark Side That Could End Badly for Investors

headline: Nvidia's outlook will be a test of its strategy to maintain AI dominance
summary: Nvidia is expected to deliver another blockbuster earnings report on Wednesday, but a shift in how artificial intelligence is used is raising doubts on how long its dominance in AI chips ‌can last.  After years of near-monopoly in chips used to train AI systems, Nvidia is facing competition from tech giants building their ‌own chips to capture demand that is shifting toward processors that run AI systems, respond to queries and carry out tasks in real time.  This so-called inference market is ​much larger, but also more contested.

content.title: Nvidia's outlook will be a test of its strategy to maintain AI dominance

This so-called inference market is ​much larger, but also more contested.

headline: Vital Signals Raises $15M to Revolutionize Understanding and Management of Blood Pressure
summary: Vital Signals, a health innovation company founded by technology veteran Tom Moss, today announced that it has raised over $15M in investments, led by XYZ Ventures, to support its mission to improve how people understand, engage with and manage their blood pressure and long‑term heart health.

headline: New Research: Google's AI Overviews Now Cost Websites 58% of Their Clicks
summary: SINGAPORE, May 19, 2026--New research from Ahrefs, a leading marketing platform, found that Google's AI Overviews now result in a 58% lower average clickthrough rate for top-ranking pages, up from 34.5% just eight months ago. Despite Google’s statements that links in AI Overviews get higher clickthrough rates than if outside of it, the jump suggests that with AI Overviews in their current form, those clicks to websites won't come back.

content.title: New Research: Google's AI Overviews Now Cost Websites 58% of Their Clicks
